hard.next_step: Jun 22, 2026: Siemens RfI thank-you for myMall 2.0 participation; no reply to Dirk's follow-up since. Jun 08: strong demo, Dirk following up. RFP window Aug-Oct per RfI schedule.
hard.ais_why: Product scope includes ANA (Autonomous Negotiation Agent) and Merlin Intake, the explicit AI products in scope on a $1.58M Siemens engagement. AIS held at 5 (Moderate): no verbatim buyer AI dialogue was captured (no recorded Avoma calls), so the AI appetite is inferred from product shape, not confirmed by the buyer.

north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus cleared Siemens's myMall 2.0 RfI shortlist (30 Apr) and demoed intake/eProcurement well, but the buyer has been quiet since its procedural 10 Jun RfI thank-you and Dirk's 22 Jun follow-up drew no reply, 26 days of silence. The deal sits at evaluator-tier only: SVP Sebastian Kahlmeyer and primary Decision Maker Sara Wack have never engaged, and no one is advocating for Zycus internally. The next real gate, the RFP, is Aug-Oct, so momentum has thinned with no near-term forcing event.
north_star_verdict.math: 233 days to close (2027-02-24). Created 24 Feb 2026, qualified 27 Feb, ~130 days in Qualified stage. RfI response submitted 10 Apr, cleared RfI shortlist 30 Apr, demo delivered ~26 May, Q&A answered 29 May, Siemens RfI thank-you 10 Jun. RFP window Aug-Oct is 26-116 days out. No stage slip; amount stable at $1.58M since the 16 Apr correction from a $100k placeholder. Sole current drag is a 26-day buyer silence with no proposed next step and no engaged power tier.
evidence: Last buyer touch 10 Jun 2026 (26 days ago): Siemens sent a procedural RfI thank-you proposing no next step (SF Next_Step__c)
Dirk followed up 22 Jun with no buyer reply since (SF Next_Step__c)
Demo delivered ~26 May, Viktor Kosov sent a Q&A summary, Zycus answered 29 May (SF Tasks 26-29 May)

RFP window Aug-Oct 2026 named by Siemens (SF Next_Step__c 14 Apr)
Decision Makers Sebastian Kahlmeyer (SVP & Data Officer SCM Digitalization) and Sara Wack (primary) never engaged on any activity (SF OpportunityContactRole)
No champion identified; all engagement is evaluator-tier (Marta Pallas Escriche, Viktor Kosov, Lisa-Marie Wieselsberger)
No Avoma calls discovered (opp + account + attendee-email pulls returned zero recorded buyer calls)

metrics.narrative: Siemens stated it seeks to replace its current procurement solution (SF Next_Step__c, 8 May 2026) and is running a formal RFI (myMall 2.0) structured around five EPICs, which implies operational pain material enough to justify a multi-vendor process and a ~12-month cycle. However, the business impact has never been quantified in cost, headcount, cycle time, error rate, or savings, and with no recorded buyer call there is no verbatim articulation of the financial magnitude. The strongest metric signal is process weight, not a number: the disciplined RfI timeline and $1.58M scale imply exec-level budget authorization. Quantifying the replacement cost and target savings with an evaluator before the RFP is the move to convert qualitative pain into a defensible business case.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 8 May 2026: 'Siemens seeks to replace their current solution'
SF Next_Step__c 14 Apr 2026: five EPICs / RfI schedule
SF Next_Step__c 20 Apr 2026: RFP response and indicative pricing submitted

economic_buyer.narrative: Sebastian Kahlmeyer (Senior Vice President & Data Officer, SCM Digitalization) is the only contact with a seniority consistent with owning a $1.58M budget and holds a Decision Maker role in Salesforce, so he is the inferred economic buyer, but he has never appeared on any email, task, or demo. Sara Wack is the PRIMARY Decision Maker but her title (SCM PI IT S) reads functional/IT rather than budget-holding, and she too has never engaged. Every recorded interaction is with the evaluator tier (Marta Pallas Escriche, Viktor Kosov, Lisa-Marie Wieselsberger), so there is no validation of budget, commercial appetite, or executive sponsorship. The indicative pricing submitted 20 Apr sits unacknowledged by any budget owner. Securing an introduction to Kahlmeyer or Wack before the Aug RFP is the critical open move.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Sebastian Kahlmeyer, SVP & Data Officer SCM Digitalization, Decision Maker
SF OpportunityContactRole: Sara Wack, SCM PI IT S, Decision Maker, PRIMARY
SF Next_Step__c 20 Apr 2026: indicative pricing submitted, no EB acknowledgement

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are visible from the RfI structure and Zycus's five prep deliverables: SAP integration architecture, AI governance documentation, a cybersecurity compliance pack, a demo storyline aligned to five EPICs, and the SCM STAR (SCM STAR is Siemens's supplier/sourcing portal) response (SF Next_Step__c, 14 Apr 2026). Viktor Kosov's post-demo Q&A summary (26 May) and Zycus's written answers (29 May) confirm that solution fit for intake, catalog management, and eProcurement is being actively scrutinized. These are inferred-to-confirmed from the RfI mechanics rather than a buyer statement of weighted criteria, and with no recorded call the relative weighting (AI capability vs SAP integration vs security) is unknown. Extracting the RFP scoring criteria from Marta or Viktor is the way to sharpen positioning.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 14 Apr 2026: SCM STAR, AI governance, SAP integration, cybersecurity, EPIC-aligned demo
SF Task 26 May 2026: Viktor Kosov Q&A summary
SF Task 29 May 2026: Zycus written answers to pending questions

decision_process.narrative: Siemens is running a transparent, multi-stage procurement process with a published myMall 2.0 timeline: RfI issued 27 Feb, provider confirmation 6 Mar, Q&A 13 Mar, merged answers 27 Mar, offer submission 10 Apr, shortlist feedback 30 Apr, demo sessions May-June, and the RFP window Aug-Oct (SF Next_Step__c, 14 Apr 2026). Zycus passed the RfI shortlist gate (30 Apr) and delivered a demo (~26 May) that Dirk logged as strong. The process is controlled by the evaluator team (Marta Pallas Escriche coordinating, Viktor Kosov driving the Q&A) with two Decision Makers (Kahlmeyer, Wack) named but not yet active. The gap is the internal approval chain above the evaluators: who signs off the $1.58M and whether an SI/partner is required for implementation is not yet documented.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 14 Apr 2026: full RfI/RFP schedule
SF Next_Step__c 30 Apr 2026: RfI shortlist feedback
SF OpportunityContactRole: evaluator and Decision Maker roster

paper_process.narrative: The deal is still in the RfI/demo phase, so contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal redlines, InfoSec gate, procurement channel) are not yet visible. What is known is that Siemens procurement runs a compliance-heavy process: AI governance documentation, a cybersecurity compliance pack, and SAP integration validation are formal RfI gates, signalling that InfoSec and AI-governance review will be substantive downstream (SF Next_Step__c, 14 Apr 2026). A separate Certinal market-sounding thread (10 Jun) shows Siemens also engages Zycus's e-signature product, which could later ease the signature step. The move that creates paper-process visibility is asking, during RFP prep, whether Siemens requires an SI partner and what the post-RFP legal/InfoSec sequence and contract timeline look like.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 14 Apr 2026: AI governance, cybersecurity, SAP integration gates
SF Task 10 Jun 2026: Certinal market-sounding participation thread

identify_pain.narrative: Siemens stated on 8 May 2026 that it seeks to replace its current procurement solution (SF Next_Step__c), confirming dissatisfaction with the incumbent platform, but the specific operational or financial consequences (cycle time, adoption, error rate, cost) are undocumented and no recorded buyer call exists to quote them. The RfI's five EPICs and the intake / catalog management / buying scope imply the pain centres on fragmented intake-to-procure workflows and catalog control across indirect spend. The pain most plausibly sits with SCM Digitalization under Sebastian Kahlmeyer, given the myMall 2.0 branding and SVP-level Decision Maker sponsorship, but that owner has never engaged directly. Naming the concrete broken workflow and the cost of inaction with an evaluator is the open move.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 8 May 2026: 'Siemens seeks to replace their current solution'
SF OpportunityContactRole: Sebastian Kahlmeyer, SVP SCM Digitalization
SF Next_Step__c 14 Apr 2026: five EPICs / intake-catalog-buying scope

champion.narrative: No champion has been identified. All engagement is evaluator-tier: Marta Pallas Escriche coordinates the RfI logistics, Viktor Kosov drove the post-demo Q&A (26 May) and received Zycus's answers (29 May), and Lisa-Marie Wieselsberger participated in the process, but none has advocated for Zycus internally, coached the deal, or opened access to the Decision Makers. The demo was logged as strong, yet Siemens's 10 Jun RfI thank-you was procedural with no forward step, and Dirk's 22 Jun follow-up went unanswered, a pattern of evaluators who are cooperative but not selling Zycus upward. Marta and Viktor are the most viable champion candidates; converting one into a coached advocate before the Aug RFP is the highest-leverage relationship move on this deal.
sources: SF Task 26 May 2026: Viktor Kosov Q&A
SF Next_Step__c 10 Jun 2026: procedural RfI thank-you, no next step
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026: no reply to Dirk's follow-up
SF OpportunityContactRole: no Champion role recorded

competition.narrative: Siemens is running a formal multi-vendor RfI (myMall 2.0), and Zycus cleared the shortlist on 30 Apr 2026 into the demo round, so at least two to three rivals were in the field, but their identities have never been disclosed and Competitors__c is blank with no competitor named in any email, task, or Next Step. This is a genuine knowledge gap, not a sole-source situation. The Certinal thread is Zycus's own e-signature product in a separate market-sounding phase, not a rival. Working backward from the RfI gates, SAP integration and AI governance are likely battlegrounds, but that is inference, not evidence. Asking Marta or Viktor directly who else advanced and how Zycus compares before the RFP is the move to close the gap.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 30 Apr 2026: RfI shortlist for demo sessions
SF Competitors__c: null
SF Task 10 Jun 2026: Certinal (own product) market-sounding thread, not a competitor

deal_movement.summary: Steady RfI progression from Feb through late May, then a stall: Zycus cleared the shortlist and demoed well, but the buyer went quiet after a procedural 10 Jun thank-you and has not replied to Dirk's 22 Jun follow-up. No stage slip; the next real gate (RFP) is Aug-Oct.

competitive_position.summary: No rival has been named in any source: Siemens ran a multi-vendor RfI (myMall 2.0) and Zycus cleared the 30 Apr shortlist into demos, but the other shortlisted vendors' identities were never disclosed and Competitors__c is blank. This is a competitive knowledge gap, not a sole-source; the field will surface at the Aug-Oct RFP. The Certinal thread is Zycus's own e-signature product, not a competitor. The real current threat is invisibility, not a named rival: with no champion, Zycus has no internal read on how it placed.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Siemens's evaluation criteria are set by the RfI: SAP integration, AI governance, cybersecurity compliance, and intake/catalog/eProcurement fit against five EPICs. Zycus has responded to each on paper, but none is buyer-validated yet, so positions read partially aligned to exposed pending RFP scrutiny.
